preamble: Debuccalization of [b] to [h] typically occurs through an intermediate fricative stage [β], often in intervocalic positions where the oral closure is lost.

examples.note: Complex allophonic and gendered variation resulting in glottal transitions.

sources: Crystal, D. (2008). A Dictionary of Linguistics and Phonetics (6th ed.). Blackwell Publishing.
Everett, D. L. (1986). Pirahã. In Derbyshire, D. C. & Pullum, G. K. (eds.), Handbook of Amazonian Languages, Vol. 1, pp. 200–325. Mouton de Gruyter.
